title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Go Client Systems · Contract Triage
summary: An on-call reliability engineer tracing a production client incident must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for https, tls identity, certificate trust, proxies, and downgrade boundaries, producing transport trust and certificate evidence record. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Go 1.26 net/http transport trust and certificate evidence record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regres…
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Preserve hostname, chain, validity, algorithm, and revocation-policy checks without disabling verification. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Https Identity
steps.instruction:
debug case — Go incident collection agent connects to api3.internal.test through proxy.example.test using a private CA and optional client certificate. Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Inspect the Https Identity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Https Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind https authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Https Identity, keep Https Identity connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
